topStories.description: Hezbollah has been launching attacks in response to US-Israel war on Iran as more Israeli troops enter southern Lebanon.

topStories.description: Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge surpassed movies like Hoppers, Reminders of Him, and GOAT on the USA box office list on March 19.

topStories.title: Modi calls ministerial meeting today to review petroleum, power and fertiliser sectors amid US-Iran war — What we know
topStories.description: PM Narendra Modi will hold a ministerial meeting to discuss the energy situation and status of petroleum, power, and fertiliser sectors, amid the US-Iran war in West Asia. Focus will be on logistics and supply stability.

topStories.title: Fireball Seen Blazing Across Texas Sky As Meteor Explodes Midair
topStories.description: Due to its intense brightness, high velocity, and the subsequent audible sounds, this event successfully captured the public's attention.

dailyNews.title: Punjab official suicide: Opposition steps up pressure for Laljit Singh Bhullar’s arrest
dailyNews.description: Congress, BJP, and SAD demand arrest of former AAP minister after warehousing corporation official’s death by suicide

Puerto Rican activists bring medicine to Havana despite US blockade
dailyNews.description: Puerto Rican activists brought suitcases filled with medicine and medical supplies to Havana despite US blockade.

dailyNews.title: Middle East Conflict: Israeli PM Netanyahu urges global leaders to join the war with US against Iran
dailyNews.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday (local time) called on the global leaders to join hands with Israel and the US in their war against Iran, citing Tehran's recent attacks on Tel Aviv.

dailyNews.title: Meteor breaks apart mid-air over Texas, triggers loud booms | Watch
dailyNews.description: As per the space agency, residents reported seeing the luminous object at around 4:40 p.m. CDT, describing it as a streak racing across the sky. The meteor first became visible at an altitude of roughly 49 miles above Stagecoach, located northwest of Houston.

dailyNews.title: US-Israel, Iran conflict: HMS Anson, Britain's nuclear-powered submarine, arrives in the Arabian Sea, says report
dailyNews.description: HMS Anson, Britain's nuclear-powered submarine has arrived in the northern Arabian Sea as tensions in the Middle East soar. Equipped with Tomahawk missiles, the Royal Navy submarine left a port from Australia's Perth on 6 March, days after the US and Israel launched strikes against Iran

dailyNews.title: Iran claims it has shot down ‘invading’ US F-15 fighter jet near Hormuz Island: Watch video
dailyNews.description: Iran claims to have shot down an enemy F-15 fighter jet near Hormuz Island, with investigations ongoing. The US and other Middle Eastern nations have not commented on the situation, while Iranian media has released a video of the incident.

dailyNews.title: PM Modi chairs high-level energy meet, reviews power readiness as Trump warns attack over Hormuz Strait
dailyNews.description: Narendra Modi reviewed key energy sectors as crude prices reached $119 high, tensions in West Asia continued to persist.

dailyNews.title: ‘Colonialism did its job of isolating us’ – Colombia VP
dailyNews.description: Francia Marquez called for stronger Latin America–Africa ties to boost growth and challenge colonial legacies.

dailyNews.title: Latin American presidents criticise US actions
dailyNews.description: Latin American leaders met at the CELAC Summit in Colombia where President Petro called for a Middle East ceasefire.

dailyNews.title: US-operated Patriot missile likely involved in Bahrain blast on March 9 that injured over 30 people
dailyNews.description: A blast in Bahrain's Mahazza, injuring over 30 civilians, may have resulted from a U.S. Patriot missile intercepting an Iranian drone. Analysis suggests the missile detonated mid-air, causing extensive damage, while Bahrain's defenses failed to prevent the Iranian attack on a nearby oil refinery.

dailyNews.title: ‘The United States doesn’t even pretend to be within international law’
dailyNews.description: ‘The United States doesn't even pretend to be within international law’

dailyNews.title: Trump Is Finally Eyeing an Exit From Iran. But Will He Take It?
dailyNews.description: President Trump says he is considering “winding down” operations in Iran. But many of his original war goals remain unaccomplished.

dailyNews.title: As War Disrupts India’s Gulf Ties, Economy Faces ‘New Broadside’
dailyNews.description: Generations of Indian workers and businesses have turned the Arab side of the Gulf into a vital appendage of their own country.

dailyNews.title: Israel Orders Military to Intensify Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
dailyNews.description: Defense Minister Israel Katz instructed troops to destroy more bridges and buildings in southern Lebanon, stoking worries that Israel could expand a military-controlled buffer zone there.

dailyNews.title: Mullin Explored Bipartisan Deal to Rein in Immigration Crackdown
dailyNews.description: The G.O.P. senator President Trump chose to lead the Department of Homeland Security privately discussed concessions the White House has repeatedly rejected.

dailyNews.title: News Outlets Pressure Pentagon to Restore Access After Court Ruling
dailyNews.description: A federal judge ruled on Friday that key parts of the Pentagon’s press policy were unconstitutional.

topStories.title: Trump says ICE will deploy to airports Monday to assist TSA amid funding standoff
topStories.description: President Donald Trump will deploy ICE agents to airports across the country on Monday in support of TSA agents amid a funding battle with Democrats.

topStories.title: Pence: Trump upended ‘some aspects’ of GOP agenda but ‘hasn't really changed the Republican Party’
topStories.description: Mike Pence says preserving conservative principles amid rising GOP populism is the calling of his life, ahead of his new book on the conservative agenda.

topStories.description: President Trump's threat comes after CENTCOM's commander said that Iran's ability to threaten the Strait of Hormuz had been "degraded."

dailyNews.description: President Trump's threat comes after CENTCOM's commander said that Iran's ability to threaten the Strait of Hormuz had been "degraded."

dailyNews.title: Cory Booker: "We, the people, are the heroes this country needs"
dailyNews.description: The New Jersey Senator has been a face of the Democratic Party's resistance to the Trump administration. He expresses his hopes for our nation in a new book, "Stand," in which he encourages Americans to stand together, reminding us of our shared virtues.

dailyNews.title: In conversation with Cory Booker
dailyNews.description: New Jersey Senator Cory Booker has been a face of the Democratic Party's resistance to the Trump administration – from a record-breaking filibuster critical of the president's policies, to calls for accountability over U.S. involvement in the war with Iran. He also expresses his hopes for our nation in a new book, "Stand," in which he encourages Americans to stand together, reminding us of our shared virtues. He talks with Faith Salie about a political career forged in Newark, N.J. ("the toughest place in politics ever"), and how it is time to "seize, reclaim, and redeem the dream of America."

dailyNews.title: The prospects for "boots on the ground" to seize Iran's enriched uranium
dailyNews.description: As the war with Iran enters its fourth week, much of its stockpile of enriched uranium is believed to lie buried deep inside the Isfahan Mountain complex. CBS News national security correspondent David Martin talks with physicist David Albright, president of the Institute for Science and International Security, about the risks of seizing that near-weapon grade material.

dailyNews.title: Signs of the times: Removing stories of America's past from our national parks
dailyNews.description: Following an executive order from President Trump, the Department of the Interior has removed dozens of educational signs at our national parks that the administration claims promote "divisive narratives" and "corrosive ideology."

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens attack on Iran power plants if Strait of Hormuz isn't reopened
dailyNews.description: Iran and Israel traded attacks on Saturday as the war in the Middle East escalated into a dangerous new direction at the start of its fourth week.

dailyNews.title: Analysis: Trump's unshackled presidency puts him at the center of the economy
dailyNews.description: The president's unprecedented use of executive power has made him a driving force behind oil prices, the Federal Reserve's interest rates and more.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighing options for deploying thousands of ground troops in Iran
dailyNews.description: WASHINGTON — President Donald Trump is weighing whether to send possibly thousands of U.S. troops into Iran as he looks for a way to achieve some of his key goals and end the war, according to the two current U.S. officials, two former U.S. officials and another person familiar with the discussions

dailyNews.title: Intelligence chiefs' testimony is out of sync with Trump's talking points
dailyNews.description: For the first time since the U.S. launched a war on Iran, this week top intelligence officials spoke publicly about the conflict.

dailyNews.title: Cuban deputy FM reacts to Trump’s threats to take over Cuba: Full interview
dailyNews.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Cuban Deputy Foreign Minister Carlos Fernández de Cossío says “our military is always prepared” but “we don't pose any threat to the United States” after President Trump threatened a U.S. takeover of Cuba.

dailyNews.title: Flyers react to Trump’s plan for federal immigration agents
dailyNews.description: Flyers at Newark Liberty International Airport in Newark, N.J., had mixed reactions to President Donald Trump's plan to send federal immigration agents to airports amid the Department of Homeland Security shutdown. Lawmakers on Capitol Hill have been working through the weekend to fund TSA and alleviate staff squeezes, but a bill has not been passed yet. NBC New York’s Charles Watson reports.

dailyNews.title: Iran refuses to back down after Trump issues 48-hour deadline over Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Tehran has threatened to escalate strikes on energy infrastructure and target critical water desalination facilities should President Donald Trump make good on a promise to “obliterate” the country’s power plants if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz

dailyNews.title: Museum sues Trump administration alleging it canceled grant on basis of race
dailyNews.description: An Underground Railroad museum in upstate New York alleged in a lawsuit Friday that the Trump administration unlawfully terminated its federal grant on the basis of race, pointing to President Donald Trump's efforts to dismantle diversity-focused initiatives.
